data.title: UK's Clarkson tops FTSE midcaps on upbeat annual outlook
data.description:
** Shares in shipping services firm Clarkson up 7.7% at 4,190p

** Stock top pct. gainer on FTSE Midcap (.FTMC) index

** Company expects results for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024 to be slightly ahead of market expectations

** London-based firm forecasts underlying profit before tax not less than 115 million pounds ($141.37 million) for the year; it reported 109.2 mln pounds in 2023

** CKN stock hits highest since Aug. 2, 2024

** Shares had gained ~25% in 2024

($1 = 0.8135 pounds)

data.title: Rheinmetall to Secure Controlling Stake in Software Developer Blackned
data.description:
Rheinmetall agreed to buy a further 11% stake in blackned GmbH to secure a controlling stake in the software developer.

The German defense technology company's interest in blackned will increase to 51% following the transaction, according to a Friday release. The deal is subject to approval from the German Federal Cartel Office and other conditions.

The target company specializes in software for the digitalization of armed forces.

data.title: French Stocks Poised for Strong Weekly Gains
data.description:
The CAC 40 rose 0.3% to 7,510 on Friday, extending its gains from the previous session and staying close to a one-month high.

Investors are now looking ahead to the upcoming US jobs report for further insights into the Federal Reserve's policy direction, especially after its cautious stance on rates amid ongoing uncertainty over President Trump's economic policies.

Meanwhile, in France, industrial production rose by 0.2% month-on-month in November 2024, rebounding from a revised 0.3% decline in the previous month and exceeding forecasts of flat growth.

Among individual stocks, luxury stocks were among the top performers, with LVMH, Hermes, and Kering rising by 0.8%, 0.8%, and 1.3%, respectively.

Other notable gainers included Michelin, Airbus, ArcelorMittal, and Capgemini, with their shares increasing between 1.1% and 1.9%.

Over the week, the index is set to post strong gains.

data.title: Mercedes-Benz Vehicle Sales Fell 4% in 2024 Amid Challenging Market
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

Mercedes-Benz car and van sales fell 4% to 2.39 million vehicles in 2024 amid a challenging global market, with top-end vehicle sales weighed by market conditions in China, model changes and weak electric-vehicle demand.

Sales fell 2% in the final quarter of 2024 compared with the corresponding quarter of 2023.

The luxury German auto maker sold 625,800 cars and vans in the fourth quarter as top-end car sales rose 1% on the year while sales of its core models rose 10%. However, its entry-level models saw a 15% sales drop.

"We finished the year with a strong sales quarter, especially with our top-end vehicles and the G-Class in particular," said Britta Seeger, member of the board of management of Mercedes-Benz Group Marketing & Sales.

By region, car sales in North America rose 11% in the quarter on the year, while sales grew 3% in China and fell 5% in Europe.

Battery-electric car sales fell 26% in the quarter, it said.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: India's CESC falls after reporting lower Q3 profit
data.description:
** Shares of India's CESC fell as much as 4% before paring some losses, last down 3%

** Power generator and distributor's Q3 consol net profit fell 5.7% y/y to 2.65 bln rupees ($30.8 mln)

** Stock set for third straight session of decline

** CESC set for worst weekly performance since March 2020

** Stock climbed 40.3% in 2024

($1 = 85.9375 Indian rupees)

data.title: Bulgaria Construction Output Growth Accelerates to 8.6%
data.description:
Construction output in Bulgaria increased by 8.6% year-on-year in November 2024, quickening from a downwardly revised 6% growth in the previous month.

Building construction (5.9% vs 4.2% in October) and civil engineering works (8.8% vs 6.2%) continued to rise.

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, construction output rose by 1.8% in November, following an upwardly revised 0.2% gain in October.

data.title: Malaysian Shares Edge Higher; KLCI Likely to Consolidate Further — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's Kuala Lumpur Composite Index ended 0.1% higher at 1602.41. The benchmark index is likely consolidate further, as technical analysis point to continued weakening after it fell below key support levels, Hong Leong IB analyst Ng Jun Sheng says in a note. Investors remained cautious ahead of Trump's policies to be announced after his inauguration, a potentially hawkish Fed, persistent foreign outflows, China's economic struggles and looming central bank decisions, he adds. Ng pegs the KLCI's support at 1563-1586 and resistance at 1611-1623. Among gainers, YTL Corp. and YTL Power International rose 1.2% each, while Petronas Dagangan fell 3.0% and Axiata Group lost 1.7%. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Gold Set for Weekly Gains on U.S. Policy Uncertainty — Market Talk
data.description: Gold is headed for a weekly gain of roughly 2% as uncertainty around future U.S. policy under President-elect Donald Trump boosts safe-haven demand. In early trade, futures are up 0.6% to $2,706.70 a troy ounce, with traders awaiting a key U.S. jobs report later on Friday for more cues on the path of interest rates. According to Swissquote bank, activity on Fed funds futures suggests the next cut would be in May. But "a set of stronger-than-expected [jobs] data could flip this expectation to the 'no cut until June' side very rapidly, enhance the selloff in the U.S. treasuries and support a further appreciation of the U.S. dollar," senior analyst Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note to clients. The yellow metal is seen as a hedge against inflation, but higher interest rates reduce the appeal of holding the non-yielding asset. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Italy retail sales fall 0.4% m/m in November
data.description:
Italian retail sales fell 0.4% in November from the month before, data showed on Friday, following a 0.5% decrease in October.

Sales rose 1.1% in unadjusted year-on-year terms in November, ISTAT said.

The data are expressed in value terms and are not adjusted for consumer prices, which increased 1.5% in November from the year earlier, based on Italy's EU-harmonised index (HICP).

data.title: Oversea-Chinese Banking Buys Back SG$6 Million Worth of Shares
data.description:
Oversea-Chinese Banking bought back 350,000 shares in the open market on Thursday for SG$6.1 million or SG$17.5 apiece, according to a same-day filing.

The bank has been authorized to buy back up to 225.0 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has repurchased 12.2 million shares.

data.title: Indian Gas Exchange seeks regulatory nod to launch 3-6 month contract
data.description:
The Indian Gas Exchange (IGX) is looking for regulatory approval to launch a 3-6 month gas contract, its chief executive, Rajesh K Mediratta, said on Friday.

"We are awaiting a clearance from PNGRB (Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board)," Mediratta told reporters at a press conference.

IGX, a unit of Indian Energy Exchange , currently has intraday, dayahead, daily, and fortnightly contracts.

The pricing of the 3-6 month contract will be linked to global benchmarks and IGX's own index called GIXI.

The new long-duration contracts will have a payment cycle of a fortnight compared to the current 2-3 days, Mediratta said.

Additionally, IGX is considering introducing green certificates for trading, he said, as the government looks to make the usage of 1% compressed bio gas (CBG) mandatory for city gas sellers.

"If a city gas distributor doesn't have a CBG plant, they can buy these certificates. Trading will also encourage compressed bio gas production," he said.

IGX has also signed a preliminary agreement with Austria's Central Europeans Gas Hub to explore gas trading opportunities, including those for green gases such as hydrogen and methane.

India wants to raise its share of gas in the domestic energy mix to 15% by 2030 from 6.2% at present.

data.title: SemiLEDs 1Q Rev $1.3M >LEDS
SemiLEDs 1Q Loss/Shr 8c >LEDS
Oxley Buys Back More Shares
data.description:
Oxley Holdings bought back 200,900 shares in the open market on Thursday for SG$14,366, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The company has been authorized to buy back about 211.5 million shares under its current mandate and has repurchased roughly 2.9 million shares so far.

data.title: Stifel sees 'dark clouds' over Wacker Chemie's solar-exposed biz, shares fall
data.description:
** Stifel sees 'dark clouds over the solar business' and cuts Wacker Chemie to "hold" from "buy", with a PT nearly halved to 77 euros

** Shares in the German specialty chemicals maker fall 5.4%

** The U.S. photovoltaic market is the only one left that shows profitable margins for Wacker's solar-grade polysilicon business, which bears the risk of being structurally oversupplied, the broker says

** Stifel forecasts the company's polysilicon segment FY 2025 EBITDA at 54 million euros ($55.6 million), significantly below the consensus of 265 million it cites

** "High degree of uncertainty over future solar deliveries could result in considerable share price volatility, especially in H1 2025," the broker says

** Stifel still sees all other business segments on track for solid growth in FY 2025 and beyond, which puts its estimates for them in line with consensus

** Shares are on track for the worst day since mid-July 2024 if losses persist

($1 = 0.9708 euros)

data.title: Sentiment Among Hong Kong SMEs Improves in December 2024
data.description:
Sentiment among Hong Kong's small and medium businesses improved in the final month of 2024, according to data from the city's census and statistics department released Friday.

The diffusion index on business receipts among small and medium-sized enterprises rose to 43.9 in December 2024 from 41.9 a month prior.

The diffusion index for import and export trades rose to 45.7 from 43.1 a month earlier, while that of retail trade increased to 40.2 from 37.7 and that of business services to 48.4 from 45.9.

For January 2025, the diffusion index is expected to be at 47.

A government spokesperson said measures to improve the economy in the mainland, which would benefit Hong Kong, should help boost market and business sentiment.

data.title: Seven & i Holdings' Attributable Falls 65% in Fiscal Nine Months
data.description:
Seven & i Holdings' profit attributable to owners of the parent fell 65.1% to 63.6 billion yen in the fiscal nine months ended Nov. 30, from 182.2 billion yen a year ago. mainly due to higher expenses.

The company said earnings per share (EPS) dropped to 24.48 yen from 68.74 yen a year ago, according to a Thursday filing.

Revenue gained 5.7% to 9.07 billion yen from 8.58 billion yen a year earlier.

It forecasted attributable profit to grow 27.4% to 163 billion yen or 62.74 yen per share and revenues to gain 3.5% to 11.9 billion yen for the fiscal year ending Feb. 28, 2025.

The company expects to pay a year-end dividend of 20 yen per share for the current fiscal year.

Seven & i Holdings' shares gained nearly 5% at market close.

data.title: Bellsystem24's Nine-Month Profit Slides 22%
data.description:
Bellsystem24 Holdings recorded a 22% decline in attributable net income for the nine months ended Nov. 30, 2024, to 4.68 billion yen from 5.99 billion yen in the year-ago period, a Thursday bourse filing said.

Earnings per share stood at 63.22 yen for the three quarters, down from 80.94 yen in the corresponding period of the last fiscal year.

The CRM services provider's revenue fell by 4.5% to 107.9 billion yen in the nine months from 113 billion yen a year prior.

The company expects an attributable net income of 8 billion yen, basic EPS of 109.14 yen per share, revenue of 153 billion yen, and a total dividend of 60 yen per share for the fiscal year ending Feb. 28.

Shares of the company closed over 1% higher on Friday.

data.title: Market Chatter: Cathay Pacific Airways Apologizes After Second Consecutive Food Poisoning on Nepal Flight
data.description:
Cathay Pacific Airways apologized to passengers on two flights from Nepal following incidents of food poisoning on those flights, the South China Morning Post reported Thursday.

Hong Kong's flag carrier also promised to cooperate with authorities with their investigation, the report said.

More than a dozen passengers of CX640 from Kathmandu felt sick, with 12 of those passengers brought to a hospital in North Lantau, Hong Kong, on Thursday, the report said.

On Wednesday, 10 passengers fell sick on flight 640 also fell sick, according to the SCMP.

The airline said the food contamination came from outside the airline, adding Cathay Pacific will take care of the matter "transparently," the SCMP reported.

Shares fell 1% during Friday's afternoon trading.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Novonix Licenses Harper International's Graphitization Furnace Technology
data.description:
Novonix said late Thursday it has entered a licensing agreement with Harper International to obtain the rights and use of its continuous, induction-based graphitization furnace technology for the production of batteries.

After an initial payment within 12 months of the agreement's signing, Novonix will have the exclusive license to use the technology to further develop the furnaces used for the thermal production of graphite material for use in the battery anode market, the company said.

Once certain performance objectives are met and the company pays additional licensing fees, the deal will expand to include the right to build equipment using the licensed technology, Novonix said.

data.title: Rupee dips to lifetime low amid firm dollar ahead of key jobs data
data.description:
The Indian rupee hit an all-time low on Friday, pressured by a firm dollar and as regional currencies stayed on the defensive ahead of the keenly awaited U.S. nonfarm payrolls report.

The rupee dropped to a record low of 85.9400 per U.S. dollar, down from 85.8475 in the previous session and past the prior all-time low of 85.9325 hit on Thursday.

The dollar index was around 109.12 ahead of the jobs data, due after Indian market hours.

The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield was around 4.68% in Asia hours. It hit an over eight-month high of 4.73% earlier this week.

data.title: SemiLEDs Reports First Quarter Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
SemiLEDs Reports First Quarter Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results

HSINCHU, Taiwan--(BUSINESS WIRE)--January 10, 2025--

SemiLEDs Corporation , "SemiLEDs" or the "Company," a developer and manufacturer of LED chips and LED components, today announced its financial results for the first quarter of fiscal year 2025, ended November 30, 2024.

Revenue for the first quarter of fiscal 2025 was $1.3 million, compared to $1.3 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024. GAAP net loss attributable to SemiLEDs stockholders for the first quarter of fiscal 2025 decreased to $547 thousand, or $(0.08) per diluted share, compared to a net loss of $560 thousand, or $(0.08) per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024.

GAAP gross margin for the first quarter of fiscal 2025 increased to 21%, compared to 12% for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024. Operating margin for the first quarter of fiscal 2025 was negative 52%, compared with negative 62% for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024. The Company's cash and cash equivalents were $1.2 million at November 30, 2024, compared to $1.7 million at the end of the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024.

About SemiLEDs

SemiLEDs develops and manufactures LED chips and LED components for general lighting applications, including street lights and commercial, industrial, system and residential lighting, along with specialty industrial applications such as ultraviolet (UV) curing, medical/cosmetic, counterfeit detection, horticulture, architectural lighting and entertainment lighting. SemiLEDs sells blue, white, green and UV LED chips.

Forward Looking Statements

This press release contains statements that may constitute "forward-looking" statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and as defined in the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. All statements other than statements of historical fact could be deemed forward-looking, including, but not limited to, revenue expectations for the second quarter ending February 28, 2025, the expedited timing of the recognition of revenue relating to the buy-sell purchase orders and estimated costs of revenue related thereto, any statements about historical results that may suggest trends for SemiLEDs' business; any statements of the plans, strategies and objectives of management for future operations; any statements of expectation or belief regarding recovery of the LED industry, market opportunities and other future events or technology developments; any statements regarding SemiLEDs' position to capitalize on any market opportunities; and any statements of assumptions underlying any of the foregoing. These forward-looking statements are based on current expectations, estimates, forecasts and projections of future SemiLEDs' or industry performance based on management's judgment, beliefs, current trends and market conditions and involve risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from those contained in the forward-looking statements. SemiLEDs' Annual Report on Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") and other SemiLEDs filings with the SEC (which you may obtain for free at the SEC's website at http://www.sec.gov) discuss some of the important risks and other factors that may affect SemiLEDs' business, results of operations and financial condition. SemiLEDs undertakes no intent or obligation to publicly update or revise any of these forward looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by law.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Friday at 4 AM ET
data.description:
Los Angeles Utility Didn't Implement Common Wildfire Safety Measures

One of the utilities delivering electricity to fire-ravaged areas hasn't developed a plan to proactively shut off parts of its system during windstorms to reduce the risk of sparks from its power lines, regulatory filings show.

Oil futures end higher, with Brent turning up for the week

Oil futures gained on Thursday, with global benchmark prices turning higher for the week and buoyed by optimism over the outlook for demand.

Wildfires Are Making Investors Nervous About Los Angeles Area Utility Again

Southern California's Edison International owes its survival to luck, at least in part.

KKR, PSP Investments Partner to Buy Stake in Two American Electric Power Companies

KKR is partnering with the Public Sector Pension Investment Board to buy a 19.9% interest in two units of American Electric Power for $2.82 billion.

Constellation Energy Near Deal for Calpine

The cash-and-stock deal would value the power company at around $30 billion including debt.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on oil prices, Shell, Repsol and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Extend Decline to Seven Weeks

U.S. crude oil inventories fell for a seventh consecutive week, while product stocks saw large builds last week as refineries increased their capacity use.

Shell Warns on Earnings, Cuts Production Guidance

The energy giant said it expects earnings from its core integrated gas division to fall significantly in the fourth-quarter compared with the previous one due to expiring hedging contracts.

The Billionaire Mining Magnate Who Bet Coal Had a Future-and Won Big

The U.K. closed its last coal-powered plant, but 76-year-old Low Tuck Kwong ignored naysayers and built a fortune from the world's dirtiest fossil fuel.

ExxonMobil Earnings to Fall on Lower Oil Prices

The change in gas prices, however, may boost earnings by as much as $400 million for the quarter, the company said..

data.title: Germany's trade surplus with US reaches record high as Trump tariffs loom
data.description:
The German trade surplus with the United States is heading towards a record level just before U.S. President-elect Donald Trump arrives at the White House, an analysis of statistics office data showed.

While the German statistics office published trade data for November on Thursday, Reuters calculations of 11 month figures showed that between January and November German trade surplus with the U.S. exceeded 65 billion euros ($66.95 billion), well above the previous 63.3 billion euro record reported for the full year 2023.

"German exports to the U.S. have developed very well in recent months and are now at a record level," said Jens Suedekum from the Duesseldorf Institute for Competition Economics (DICE).

The trend could reverse, however, after Trump's inauguration on Jan. 20, as the Republican has promised to impose tariffs of 10% on global imports, in measures he says would boost U.S. manufacturing.

Last month, he also said European Union countries would face more tariffs unless the bloc stepped up imports of U.S. oil and gas.

"No European country will be as badly affected as Germany," Suedekum said. "He will erect trade barriers and ask German industrial conglomerates, such as car manufacturers, to relocate their production to the USA."

Reuters calculations showed that in the first 11 months of this year, German exports to the United States increased by 2.3% to 149.9 billion euros compared with the same period of 2023, consolidating the U.S. position as the top buyer of goods "Made in Germany".

"This is also a result of the economic boom in the U.S. and the targeted offensive of the Biden administration to re-establish modern industrial production in the U.S.," said Suedekum.

($1 = 0.9709 euros)

data.title: Invitation to press and analyst conference
data.description:
Holmen

Holmen's year-end report for January-December 2024 will be published at 07.30 CET on Friday 31 January. In view of this, you are invited to watch the online broadcast of our press and analyst conference on the same day at 09.00 CET.

Time: 31 January at 09.00 CET

Online broadcast: www.holmen.com/reports

Holmen President and CEO Henrik Sjölund, together with CFO Anders Jernhall, will present and comment on the report. The presentation will be held in English.

You may also participate in the conference by telephone, by calling:

+46 8 505 100 31 (within Sweden)

+44 207 107 06 13 (from the rest of Europe)

+1 631 570 56 13 (from the US)

Please place your call no later than 08.55 CET.

We welcome you to the online conference!

For more information, please contact:

Stina Sandell, Senior Vice President Sustainability and Communications, Holmen, +46 739 86 51 12

Holmen's business is built around the forest ecocycle and the renewable products we can create from it. With a workforce of 3 500 people, we create value for shareholders, customers and society. Holmen's net sales in 2023 amounted to just under SEK 23 billion and our shares are listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, Large Cap. Please visit holmen.com for more information.
